tweets.text: It really is a semantic difference.  Do you understand what a bank deposit is?  It's not hard currency.  It's a virtual thing that because you and only you can decide who to transfer it to you "own" it.  It has value because those who have goods and services will sell those to you by you entering an instruction that you and only you can to transfer that deposit

tweets.text:
Bucket shops 101 

"In the first place, you didn't buy or sell stock. You didn't buy 100 shares of Erie to hold or to sell at a profit... You made a wager on the price, just as you might have done on the outcome of a football game."

"You could buy on a margin of one per cent. That meant that if you had twenty dollars you could buy one hundred shares of a stock selling at twenty... If the price dropped to nineteen and seven-eighths you were wiped out automatically."

"The margin was so small that the slightest wiggle against you wiped you out. The shop just took your money. It was a mathematical certainty that you'd lose."

"The bucket shops... would see that there was a large (long ) interest in some stock. They would combine and (sell) a few thousand shares of that stock on the Real Exchange... and run the price (down) a point or two. That was enough to wipe out all the margins of the customers who were (long)"

These quotes are from 1923. 

Perps are not innovative.

tweets.text:
All of evolution is taking what isn’t yours. 

You take wood to build houses. 
You take plants to eat. 
You take seeds to farm.
You take land for agriculture.
You take metals for production.
You take animals for meat.
You take people for labour.
You take oil for energy. 

To survive requires taking what isn’t yours. To thrive requires systematizing it. 

Civilisation as a concept came to be to normalize taking and laying out protections for doing so.

Today, we call it economics.
tweets.full_text:

You take metals for production.

You take people for labour.
You take oil for energy. 

To survive requires taking what isn’t yours. To thrive requires systematizing it. 

Civilisation as a concept came to be to normalize taking and laying out protections for doing so.

Today, we call it economics.

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

thread_tweets.text:
similarly in 2020 epic gradually escalated against apple for months until it finally decided to flat-out defy the most important app store rule, tricking apple's review process and inserting a third-party payment option into the live app

epic knew full well they'll get banned for this, which is why they prepared an entire video ad campaign ahead of time to publicly kickstart their fight against apple

anthropic's recent behavior reads exactly the same way

tweets.full_text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

similar to how epic games intentionally broke app store rules in 2020, forcing apple to ban fortnite, affecting 100 million ios players

similarly in 2020 epic gradually escalated against apple for months until it finally decided to flat-out defy the most important app store rule, tricking apple's review process and inserting a third-party payment option into the live app

epic knew full well they'll get banned for this, which is why they prepared an entire video ad campaign ahead of time to publicly kickstart their fight against apple

anthropic's recent behavior reads exactly the same way

tweets.text:
If I had to stereotype my X experiences with markets:

China 🇨🇳: set on cloning me with AI, can only think of trades in short term timeframes from A-shares PTSD.

America 🇺🇸:  bullish on anything futuristic like $SPCX, don’t care about valuations

Europe 🇪🇺: from $SIVE to $SOI, cares more about water usage than the AI buildout. Somehow can only look at past 12 months. (Belgium is cool so far), looking at you France + Sweden

Korea 🇰🇷: leveraged degens. I’ve never seen a market so volatile. Equivalent of 50x hyperliquid traders but with stock markets.

Japan 🇯🇵: somehow supportive of everything, haven’t seen any Japanese person aggressively bear post and short stocks before.

Not enough data on other places yet like Latin America, but will have some soon enough ig.
tweets.full_text:
If I had to stereotype my X experiences with markets:

China 🇨🇳: set on cloning me with AI, can only think of trades in short term timeframes from A-shares PTSD.

America 🇺🇸:  bullish on anything futuristic like $SPCX, don’t care about valuations

Europe 🇪🇺: from $SIVE to $SOI, cares more about water usage than the AI buildout. Somehow can only look at past 12 months. (Belgium is cool so far), looking at you France + Sweden

Korea 🇰🇷: leveraged degens. I’ve never seen a market so volatile. Equivalent of 50x hyperliquid traders but with stock markets.

Japan 🇯🇵: somehow supportive of everything, haven’t seen any Japanese person aggressively bear post and short stocks before.

Not enough data on other places yet like Latin America, but will have some soon enough ig.

tweets.text:
Not sure what feels better right now:

1) Having been one of the very few VCs that backed @Collector_Crypt early in 2024, when all the other tIeR oNe VCs passed because they thought it was too niche to ever return venture like outcomes, and now it's one of the few winning verticals in crypto.

2) Having been one of the very few liquid investors who literally doubled down at the very bottom, when everyone declared $CARDS dead while the underlying fundamentals said the complete opposite.

I usually don't like to brag about our wins like this. But finding one of the very few big winners this market has produced in years, then playing it perfectly while everyone around you calls you an idiot, feels damn good ngl.

It's exactly these kinds of winners and the privilege of working with such talented teams that I'm here for.

And why I will never, ever give up.

Higher, for longer.

tweets.text: Sinclair's new preprint provides the first direct experimental evidence for the Information Theory of Aging: your cells don't age because they break — they age because they forget who they are. The biological blueprint is still intact. The cells just stop reading it correctly. That distinction changes the entire target: you don't repair damage. You restore data. And data can be restored.
tweets.full_text: Sinclair's new preprint provides the first direct experimental evidence for the Information Theory of Aging: your cells don't age because they break — they age because they forget who they are. The biological blueprint is still intact. The cells just stop reading it correctly. That distinction changes the entire target: you don't repair damage. You restore data. And data can be restored.

tweets.text:
> creating a truly cyberpunk chaebol-capitalism type of future, where the goverment sort of runs them but they also sort of run the government

as i’ve been discussing here for a while, this is probably inevitable imo

the US is the natural proof of concept as the vectors for industry capture of the government already exist and can be shifted to the new industries with a reallocation of capital (e.g., tech lobby already attempted to buy Trump and largely succeeded in doing so in the ways which matter)

the East Asian model will be authoritarian control where the government more or less directly controls all of the compute and AI labs with a shift towards more centrally planned economies (which may even be viable this time with AI…tbd)

the European may lean towards collective ownership, but only if those models can prove remotely sustainable versus the other two. probably won’t be unless there are governance reforms

in all events, capitalism as we know it probably fails in the next 15-50 years. a lot of people will be angry to read this, but it’s already happening

to be clear, i don’t really like this future. it’s just really hard for me to see how we avoid it
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: 1. if transacting with superintelligent models outside of the boundaries of a lab becomes difficult due to national security / ai safety concerns and so on, it will mean the Coasean boundaries of the labs will grow to encompass all interesting industry, creating a truly cyberpunk

tweets.text:
Open-weights models are going to become a bigger problem for Anthropic and OpenAI going forward.

1/ Not every task requires 150 IQ
2/ 90% cheaper models at 115 IQ are often good enough

The pushback to this has always been: well, margins will come from the best frontier models.

But what happens to revenue prospects when government blocks your frontier models??
